title: NOC 31111 – Optometrists | GTR Immigration
description: Optometrists play a vital role in primary eye care across Canada. With rising screen usage, aging demographics, and increasing awareness of preventive eye health, demand for licensed optometrists continues to grow nationwide. Classified under NOC 31111 (TEER 1), this occupation aligns with:
duties: Examine eyes and assess visual efficiency
Use ophthalmoscopes, biomicroscopes, and diagnostic equipment
Diagnose ocular diseases and vision disorders
Prescribe corrective lenses and visual aids
Provide non-surgical treatment for eye conditions
Educate patients on eye care and preventive measures
Refer patients to ophthalmologists for surgical cases
Provide pre- and post-operative care for laser or cataract surgery patients
Prescribe limited medications (as permitted by provincial regulations)
Conduct research and promote visual sciences
employment_requirements: Three years of undergraduate university studies
Completion of a 4–5-year Doctor of Optometry (OD) program
Successful completion of national board examinations
Licensing by the provincial or territorial regulatory body
Without provincial licensure, optometrists cannot independently practice in Canada.
job_outlook: This indicates a stable and steady demand, especially in:

Typical annual earnings: $27,500–$167,900 CAD, depending on province, clinic ownership, and specialization.
Ownership or partnership in private clinics can significantly increase income potential.
